tool.name: SameStr Extract
tool.description: MetaPhlAn or mOTUS marker sequences from reference genomes using BLASTN
tool.command:
## The command line tool turns input file names into sample names carried along during ## the entire samestr analysis workflow. ## We either rely on users naming inputs more or less correctly, in which case we still try to prevent ## common problems like using characters that aren't compatible with samestr sample names or ## keeping a redundant file suffix in the input name. #import re #set $ref_suffix = '.' + $reference_genomes[0].ext #for $reference in $reference_genomes: #set $ref_basename = re.sub('[^\w\-\.]', '_', $reference.element_identifier.split($ref_suffix)[0]) ln -s '$reference' '${ref_basename}${ref_suffix}' && #end for
samestr extract --input-files #for $reference in $reference_genomes: '${ref_basename}${ref_suffix}' #end for --clade '$clade' --marker-dir '$database.fields.path' --aln-program '$alignment.alignment_program' --marker-trunc-len $alignment.truncate_marker $alignment.save_marker_aln

param.name: reference_genomes
param.label: Reference genomes
param.help: Note: You can provide the genomes in uncompressed fasta or compressed fasta.gz format, but you must not mix compressed and uncompressed inputs.

param.name: database
param.label: SameStr database
param.help: SameStr database built from MetaPhlAn or mOTUS markers

param.label: Clade to align genomes against
param.help: The name must correspond exactly to that of a taxonomic clade in the selected database [e.g. t__SGB10068 for MetaPhlAn]

param.name: alignment_program
param.label: Alignment program
param.help: Program to use for alignment of marker sequences.

param.label: Marker truncation length
param.help: Number of Nucleotides to be cut from each side of a marker

param.label: Save alignment files
param.help: Keep alignment files for individual markers.

data.name: msa_file
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: Alignment of genomes across clade markers

tool.help:
@SAMESTR_INTRO@
SameStr Extract ...............
SameStr Extract obtains MetaPhlAn or mOTUS marker sequences from reference genomes using BLASTN. It extracts clade-specific marker sequences for strain-level analysis. The resulting variant profiles can be used in downstream SameStr workflow steps.
**Input Requirements**
Samestr Extract can process multiple reference genomes simultaneously. For each analysis, the following inputs must be provided:
- One or more reference genome sequences containing complete bacterial genomes - A SameStr database generated from a MetaPhlAn or mOTUS clade marker database - The exact clade identifier corresponding to the taxonomic group of interest in the selected database
**Alignment parameters**
- Alignment program: You can choose between MUSCLE (default) or MAFFT for multiple sequence alignment of marker sequences. - Marker truncation length: Number of nucleotides to trim from each side of marker sequences to remove poorly aligned regions. - Save alignment files: Optionally keep multiple sequence alignments for individual markers.
**Results and Outputs**
The tool generates two or three outputs:
- A collection of per-reference SNV profiles with respect to the selected clade, i.e., each element of the collection contains the SNV profile for one reference genome with respect to the chosen clade.
Element identifiers follow the scheme <Clade>.<Reference>, which is the naming scheme expected by SameStr Merge. - A multiple-sequence alignment of genomes across clade markers. This is a single dataset in gz-compressed fasta format. - Optionally, a collection of per-marker genome alignments, in which each element is a multiple-sequence alignment in fasta format of the input genomes against one specific marker from the chosen clade.
